prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial uses rapid close-ups, bold typography, and food assembly imagery to present a Burger King sourdough sandwich.

At the start, a brown background fills the frame with large cream-colored text reading “SOURDOUGH.” The bottom edge of the frame shows the top of a toasted bread surface. The next shot reveals a stacked sandwich rising into view: toasted sourdough bread, bacon, melted cheese, and beef patties are visible, with the “SOURDOUGH” text partly obscured behind it.

The commercial then cuts to a full-frame toasted bread texture. Large dark-brown text repeats vertically: “TOASTY / TOASTY / TOASTY.” This is followed by an image of several slices of toast leaning against one another on a pale background, with crumbs scattered below.

The focus shifts to grilled meat patties cooking over flames on a metal grill. Several patties are visible with grill marks and fire glowing underneath. Next, multiple open-faced sandwiches are arranged in a repeated pattern on a light background. Some show patties with melted yellow cheese and bacon; others show grilled patties on round bread bases. In the following shot, more sandwiches appear completed with round toasted tops placed on them.

A close-up then shows bacon and melted cheese around a small patty, with large cream text over the image reading “SIZZLING.” The next close-up shows a burger or sandwich cross-section with melted cheese, bacon, and sauce; large text reads “OOEY” at the top and “GOOEY” at the bottom. Another extreme close-up emphasizes bacon strips, melted cheese, sauce, and meat layers.

Later, a Burger King-branded cardboard crown or box-like promotional object appears in the foreground, with the red and orange Burger King logo centered on it. It lifts or moves upward to reveal a sandwich underneath on a tray. The sandwich includes toasted bread, patties, cheese, bacon, and sauce. A partial sandwich is also visible at the left edge.

The final product presentation uses a split layout. On the left, a red tray holds a sandwich and a tall cup labeled “Dr Pepper,” with ice and dark soda visible. Behind it, the brown background and partial “SOURDOUGH” text remain. On the right, a bright yellow panel shows breakfast-style sourdough sandwiches on a dark tray. The text transitions into a full final title card reading “SOURDOUGH BREAKFAST KING” in large white and dark lettering against the yellow background. Three sandwiches are displayed on a tray beneath the text, showing toasted sourdough bread, egg, cheese, bacon, and meat layers. At the bottom, the Burger King logo and “YOUR WAY” are visible, along with small fine print that is too small to read clearly in the sampled frames.
